rfc_references.title: The JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) Data Interchange Format

derived_from: RFC 8259 — The JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) Data Interchange Format, December 2017
ECMA-404 2nd Edition — The JSON Data Interchange Syntax, December 2017
Test vectors verified by running Python 3.14.3 json module on macOS/Darwin 25.3.0, 2026-03-30
notes: Python's json.dumps uses default separators (', ', ': ') which produce spaces after commas and colons. All dumps expected values in this spec use the default separators.
JSON object key order: Python 3.7+ preserves insertion order of dict. The spec test dicts have a single key to avoid order sensitivity.
RFC 8259 requires that JSON text be encoded in UTF-8, UTF-16, or UTF-32. Python's json module uses UTF-8 by default (ensure_ascii=True converts non-ASCII to \uXXXX).
NaN and Infinity are not valid JSON (RFC 8259 §6) but Python's json module accepts them by default when allow_nan=True. This spec only tests RFC-compliant behavior.
json.JSONDecodeError is a subclass of ValueError.

library.notes: json is a Python stdlib module with an optional C accelerator (_json extension module). The behavioral spec is identical regardless of which implementation is active.

json_value.description: Any value representable in JSON: null, boolean, number (int or float), string, array (list), or object (dict). RFC 8259 §3.

JSONDecodeError.description: Exception raised by json.loads() on invalid input. Subclass of ValueError. Carries msg, doc, and pos attributes.

fields.name: doc
fields.description: The JSON document that caused the error

fields.name: pos
fields.description: Start index in doc where the error was found

json.dumps.description: Serialize a Python object to a JSON-formatted string. Inverse of json.loads().

parameters.name: obj
parameters.description: Object to serialize. Must be JSON-compatible (None, bool, int, float, str, list, dict).

parameters.name: indent
parameters.description: If non-None, produce pretty-printed output with this indentation level.

parameters.name: separators
parameters.description: Custom (item_separator, key_separator) tuple. Default None uses (', ', ': ').

parameters.name: ensure_ascii
parameters.description: If True (default), escape non-ASCII characters as \uXXXX.

parameters.name: sort_keys
parameters.description: If True, sort dict keys before serializing.

postconditions: json.loads(json.dumps(obj)) == obj for all JSON-compatible obj

json.loads.description: Deserialize a JSON string or bytes to a Python object. Inverse of json.dumps().

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: JSON-formatted string or bytes to parse.

return_values.JSONDecodeError: If s is not valid JSON

invariants.description: json.dumps(None) == 'null' — Python None maps to JSON null literal (RFC 8259 §3)
json.dumps(True) == 'true' — Python True maps to JSON true literal
json.dumps(False) == 'false' — Python False maps to JSON false literal
json.dumps(0) == '0' — integer zero
json.dumps(42) == '42' — positive integer
json.dumps(-7) == '-7' — negative integer
json.dumps('hello') == '"hello"' — string is quoted
json.dumps([]) == '[]' — empty JSON array
json.dumps([1, 2, 3]) — array with default separators produces spaces after commas
json.dumps({'a': 1}) — object with default separators
json.dumps([1, 2, 3], separators=(',',':')) — compact form omits spaces
json.loads('null') == None — JSON null maps to Python None
json.loads('true') == True — JSON true maps to Python True
json.loads('false') == False
json.loads('42') == 42 — JSON number without decimal point becomes Python int
json.loads('"hello"') == 'hello'
json.loads('') raises json.JSONDecodeError — empty string is not valid JSON
json.loads('{') raises json.JSONDecodeError — truncated object is not valid JSON
json.loads('nan') raises json.JSONDecodeError — NaN is not a valid JSON value (RFC 8259 §6)
json.loads('{"a": 1} extra') raises json.JSONDecodeError — trailing non-whitespace is not valid

json_text.description: A JSON text (RFC 8259 §2) is a serialized value: null, true, false, a number, a string, an array, or an object. Encoded in UTF-8 by Python's json module. Whitespace (space, tab, LF, CR) is insignificant between tokens.

fields.name: value
fields.description: A single JSON value as defined in RFC 8259 §3-§8.

error_model.return_code_semantics: json uses Python exceptions rather than integer return codes.
error_model.error_stickiness: No stateful error model — all functions are stateless.

error_codes.name: json.JSONDecodeError
error_codes.meaning: Raised by json.loads() when the input is not valid JSON. Subclass of ValueError.
